reviewer: hand-reviewed (Opus) — Node transformers.js inference proof + desktop+mobile screenshots + conformance runner evidence

rubric.evidence: conformance (webgpu, headless no-GPU): loads-clean=pass, calls-real-inference=pass. Runnability PROVEN out-of-band in Node (transformers.js 3.7.5): AutoModelForVision2Seq loaded HuggingFaceTB/SmolVLM2-256M-Video-Instruct as SmolVLMForConditionalGeneration and generated real streamed text (single-image run, int8/WASM, 738 ms). The bundle grep confirms SmolVLMForConditionalGeneration + SmolVLMProcessor + SmolVLMImageProcessor + model_type 'smolvlm' all ship in 3.7.5 — NO version-pin needed (shared lib/webai.js 3.7.5).
rubric.notes: Genuinely a WebGPU multi-frame VLM. On a real GPU it streams a live answer; on this no-GPU runner it correctly shows the needs-WebGPU state instead of faking one. Distinct from the built SmolVLM v1 (idefics3, single image).

rubric.evidence: The multi-image path is the differentiator and was Node-verified: two images in one turn produced inputs.pixel_values.dims = [1, 2, 3, 512, 512] (batch, NUM FRAMES=2, channels, H, W) and input_ids [1,151], with output referencing 'the first image ... the second image'. The 'See inside' panel surfaces exactly this: the constructed chat template (each <image> expanded to patch tokens), the live pixel_values shape (frames × resolution), the prompt-token count, and a per-token timed stream.
rubric.notes: Expected multi-frame VLM I/O shape is explicit, legible, and matches the real tensor. Semantic sanity of a live GPU answer awaits a WebGPU device (io-shape-semantic=manual).

rubric.evidence: conformance: declares-model-id=pass (HuggingFaceTB/SmolVLM2-256M-Video-Instruct linked to its card), declares-task=pass (image-text-to-text), declares-backend=pass, declares-quantisation=pass (q4f16), inference-off-main-thread=pass (worker.js). Desktop screenshot confirms at-a-glance: 'WebGPU (required — needs a GPU + shader-f16)' and the SmolVLMForConditionalGeneration architecture line.
rubric.notes: Backend requirement is stated up front, and the page explicitly distinguishes itself from SmolVLM v1 (idefics3 vs smolvlm).

rubric.evidence: shared-auto-init-loader=pass on all pages (main + 4 use-case pages + the multi-model page's TWO loaders). Every model loads through createModelLoader — no hand-rolled Load button. Desktop screenshot shows the honest needs-WebGPU loader state with accurate accounting, no auto-download.
rubric.notes: auto-init-on-cached (warm-cache returning user) still to be exercised on a GPU device; the multi-model page uses one createModelLoader per model (VLM + LLM), each auto-initing independently.

rubric.evidence: conformance: live-status-region=pass, errors-on-the-page=pass, webgpu-capability-honest=blocked. Desktop screenshot shows the exact honest fallback: 'This model can't run on this device/browser. It needs WebGPU (no GPU adapter here). Enable it via chrome://gpu / a WebGPU-capable browser.' Generation failures are caught and shown in the .status.err region on every page.
rubric.notes: Textbook honest capability handling — needs-WebGPU with enable steps, no blank panel, no faked output. blocked (not fail) is the correct outcome on a no-GPU runner.

rubric.evidence: Real, labelled controls across the ladder: main page has a multi-frame strip (add/remove per frame with aria-labelled × buttons), sample thumbnails (role=button, keyboard), image drop/upload zone, a video → frame sampler (HTMLVideoElement + canvas), prompt input, 5 quick-prompt chips, frames-per-video + max-tokens number inputs, Generate. Wild page animates a canvas scene and samples frames; practical has A/B slots; multi-model has tone select + two-stage run. buttons-have-names=pass. controls-operable=manual.
rubric.notes: Controls are correctly disabled until the model is ready (honest on a no-GPU device). A full keyboard/focus pass belongs on a WebGPU device where controls enable.

rubric.evidence: Desktop screenshot: the answer area shows the placeholder 'The model's answer will stream here.' and the loader shows the needs-WebGPU state — nothing is fabricated as model output. All output flows through the real worker inference path (or an honest failure/unsupported state).
rubric.notes: Confirmed no canned answer on any of the five pages.

rubric.evidence: conformance: images-have-alt=pass (samples + per-frame thumbnails have descriptive alt), buttons-have-names=pass. Sample thumbs and drop zones are labelled role=button with Enter/Space handlers; answer + status use aria-live; the canvas scene has an aria-label; per-frame remove buttons carry aria-label 'Remove frame N'.
rubric.notes: Strong. Screen-reader wording of the streaming token panel could be reviewed on a GPU device.

rubric.evidence: responsive-check matrix: desktop (1280×800) and mobile (360×740 DPR3) both automated-clean — no horizontal overflow, no clipped controls, console + network clean. Desktop screenshot shows the two-column image/answer grid; mobile screenshot shows a clean single-column reflow with the model id wrapping (word-break) and no overflow. Flex grid uses flex-wrap + min-inline-size:0 per css-layout guidance.
rubric.notes: Layout parity good on both classes. Because the interactive path is WebGPU-gated, a full mobile interaction pass needs a mobile GPU device; the unsupported-state layout is clean on both. Support recorded needs-review (automated-only signal).

rubric.evidence: All inference runs in Web Workers with token streaming (main VLM worker + a separate narrator LLM worker for multi-model); the 'See inside' panel surfaces per-token arrival timing. Video-frame sampling uses async seeks (event-driven, never a blocking loop); the wild-page canvas animation uses requestAnimationFrame and respects prefers-reduced-motion. inp-stays-low=manual.
rubric.notes: Streaming + off-thread generation is INP-friendly by construction; measure tok/s + INP on a real GPU. The multi-model page runs two large WebGPU models — measure combined memory on a real device.

guidanceConsulted.recommendation: Use flexbox for single-axis control rows with flex-wrap so items reflow on narrow viewports; set min-inline-size:0 on flex items with long unbreakable content to prevent overflow; use flex: 1 1 <basis> rather than fixed widths.
guidanceConsulted.appliedOrException: applied — .vlm-grid / .controls-row use display:flex; flex-wrap:wrap; gap; every column is flex:1 1 <basis> with min-inline-size:0, and the at-a-glance model id uses word-break. Result: zero horizontal overflow on desktop AND 360px mobile (responsive-check clean on both).

guidanceConsulted.recommendation: Keep heavy work off the main thread / yield so it doesn't spike INP; avoid long synchronous loops.
guidanceConsulted.appliedOrException: applied — SmolVLM2 + the narrator LLM both run in Web Workers and stream tokens incrementally; video-frame sampling is driven by async 'seeked' events (no synchronous decode loop); the multi-model page captions frames sequentially via awaited worker calls.
guidanceConsulted.evidence: conformance inference-off-main-thread=pass; worker.js + worker-llm.js; sampleVideoFrames uses event-driven seeks in smolvlm2.js

guidanceConsulted.recommendation: Keep long generation loops off the main thread and surface progress so interaction stays responsive.
guidanceConsulted.appliedOrException: applied — worker-based streaming with a live token stream + per-token timing readout; controls are disabled until onReady to avoid mid-load interaction jank; the canvas preview uses requestAnimationFrame and honours prefers-reduced-motion.
guidanceConsulted.evidence: See-inside token stream + readout; disabled Generate until onReady; wild/index.html rAF + reduced-motion branch

openQuestions: On a WebGPU device: does SmolVLM2 produce a coherent answer that genuinely references EACH frame (e.g. 'the first frame … the last frame'), streaming token by token across 2–6 frames?

Is the multi-frame demo usable on a mobile WebGPU device given two 512×512 frames' worth of image tokens plus (on the multi-model page) a second 0.5B LLM — or should mobile be recorded unsupported with a memory-requirement note?
Does the video-frame sampler behave well for long or high-resolution clips on a real device (seek latency, memory), and should it cap resolution/duration more aggressively?
On the multi-model page, does loading TWO WebGPU models (VLM ~250 MB + Qwen 0.5B ~500 MB) fit in typical GPU memory, and is the sequential-caption stage fast enough to feel live?

followUpGoals.goal: GPU-device matrix pass for smolvlm2-video: confirm streaming multi-frame output + all controls on desktop WebGPU; assess mobile WebGPU memory feasibility (single-model pages AND the two-model multi-model page), then set support (ok, or mobile:unsupported+memory-evidence).
Add a live-camera 'what just happened?' wild demo that buffers the last N camera frames and asks SmolVLM2 to reason over them (camera copilot), building on the frame-sequence path.

summary: smolvlm2-video is a genuine in-browser MULTI-FRAME VLM, distinct from the built SmolVLM v1 (idefics3, single image): it loads HuggingFaceTB/SmolVLM2-256M-Video-Instruct as SmolVLMForConditionalGeneration on the shared 3.7.5 pin (no version-pin needed) and takes several images/video-frames in one turn — Node-verified end to end (real streamed output; two-image pixel_values [1,2,3,512,512]). The five-page ladder (single describe → A/B compare → canvas frame-sequence 'what changed' → VLM→LLM narration) each runs real off-main-thread inference through createModelLoader with a live 'See inside' surface. On this no-GPU runner it shows a clear needs-WebGPU explanation with enable steps and never a faked answer (webgpu assertion correctly blocked, not failed). Runtime disclosure, accessibility, and desktop+mobile layout parity are strong; the semantic-output + full interaction checks and a mobile-WebGPU memory-feasibility call need a GPU device before support flips from needs-review. Revision 1.
